ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Continued To Jun-02-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Miller for and with (Trustee) Dat Huynh and (Guardian ad Litem) Loi Huynh.

The Court stated that it appears everything has been spent without a court order, most on improper expenditures, that there are issues regarding notice being given and an issue as to whether it is proper for an attorney to represent both the Guardian ad Litem (GAL) and Trustee. Mr. Miller proposed that the attorney who drafted the Special Needs Trust (SNT) represent the Trustee, that the attorney has agreed to do so, that notice was not given and they will need to refile and requests a 60 day continuance to work on the examiners requests. The Court further stated that there was a bond, that there is an issue as to whether the amounts need to be restored, that the funeral expenses paid for another person were not proper, that there are serious problems and will grant a short continuance to get the representation issues ironed out. The Accounting petition was continued to June 2, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The First Account
Off Calendar Feb-18-2015 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Miller for Loi Huynh. Dat Huynh (Son) also present.

Mr. Miller stated that he is assisting Trustee Dat Huynh who is acting on behalf of his Father. The Court clarified that Dat Huynh is not the Trustee, informed the Parties that an accounting which is now overdue is needed, stated that there was no appearance on November 26, 2014 and that there is no record of Mr. Miller withdrawing. The Court also stated that it appears the funds in the special needs trust ("SNT") have already been spent, that there are limitations on special needs trusts to how funds are spent, and that the Father is responsible for this and has to get it done. Mr. Miller stated that he received a list of expenses along with receipts and that he is willing to assist in getting an accounting in proper form. The order to appear is withdrawn. Status on filing of the account was continued to February 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lelu. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The First Account
Off Calendar Jan-07-2015 Continued To Feb-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The First Account
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Continued To Jan-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney to appear on January 7, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear and why the first account has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Bond
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Off Calendar

Notice Of Petition And Petition For Order Authorizing Establishment Of Special Needs Trust
Off Calendar Sep-25-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jenella Sambour for and with Loi Huynh (Petitioner). Dat Huynh (Proposed Trustee/Son of Loi) also present.

Dat Huynh reported as to his Mother's health condition. The Court stated that the Petition to Establish a Special Needs Trust appears to be in order and granted said petition (subject to receipt and review of a proposed order witch is to have the Trust attached). Bond in the amount of $47,000.00 is required. Status on filing of bond was set for October 2, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the first account was set for November 26, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court advised Counsel that it usually does not like to waive trust requirements and denied the Petition for Waiver of Trust Requirements Under CRC 7.903. Counsel was also advised to provide two separate orders (one for each petition) and that the special needs trust order is to reflect the above status hearing dates. The Court also directed Counsel to provide guidance to her Cleint as to how Trust funds are to be used.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition For Waiver Of Trust Requirements Under Cal.Rule Of Court 7.903(C)(4-6)
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Denied
